content.text: Found the leaked token. I need to scrub it from the session logs, then force-amend the commit and push.

arguments.command:
cd /Users/landonking/.openclaw && \
TOKEN='ghp_[REDACTED_PUSH_PROTECTION]' && \
REDACTED='ghp_[REDACTED_PUSH_PROTECTION]' && \
for f in agents/main/sessions/19132602-a4b3-4a6f-8dac-10bdf1fdedbf.jsonl agents/main/sessions/19132602-a4b3-4a6f-8dac-10bdf1fdedbf.trajectory.jsonl; do
  if [ -f "$f" ]; then
    sed -i '' "s/$TOKEN/$REDACTED/g" "$f"
    echo "Scrubbed: $f"
  fi
done

content.text: Another session file has the same token. Let me do a broader scan and scrub all files at once.

arguments.command:
cd /Users/landonking/.openclaw && \
TOKEN='ghp_[REDACTED_PUSH_PROTECTION]' && \
# Find ALL files containing the token
grep -rl "$TOKEN" . --include='*.jsonl' --include='*.json' --include='*.md' 2>/dev/null

content.text:
./agents/main/sessions/46952611-434e-4636-ac4d-a8735da71f5f.jsonl
./logs/cache-trace.jsonl

arguments.command:
cd /Users/landonking/.openclaw && \
TOKEN='ghp_[REDACTED_PUSH_PROTECTION]' && \
REDACTED='ghp_[REDACTED_PUSH_PROTECTION]' && \
for f in agents/main/sessions/46952611-434e-4636-ac4d-a8735da71f5f.jsonl logs/cache-trace.jsonl; do
  if [ -f "$f" ]; then
    sed -i '' "s/$TOKEN/$REDACTED/g" "$f"
    echo "Scrubbed: $f"
  fi
done && \
# Also do a broad sweep for any other ghp_ tokens that might be in other session files
grep -rl 'ghp_' agents/ --include='*.jsonl' 2>/dev/null | while read f; do
  # Replace any full ghp_ tokens (40+ chars)
  perl -i -pe 's/ghp_[A-Za-z0-9]{36,}/ghp_[REDACTED_PUSH_PROTECTION]/g' "$f"
  echo "Perl-scrubbed: $f"
done
